action_set_definitions.name: get_satellite_telemetry
action_set_definitions.description: Retrieves the current orbital and system status including altitude, velocity, fuel reserves, battery levels, and orientation. This must be used to initialize the mission state and monitor progress.

action_set_definitions.name: align_to_prograde
action_set_definitions.description: Adjusts the satellite's attitude to a specific quaternion. Aligning to the prograde vector is critical for maximizing the efficiency of orbital raising maneuvers, ensuring the highest altitude gain per kilogram of fuel.

target_quaternion.description: The orientation [w, x, y, z] to align the satellite thrusters with the velocity vector.

action_set_definitions.name: execute_thrust_burn
action_set_definitions.description: Fires the main engines for a specified duration. Note: Longer burns are generally more fuel-efficient than multiple short pulses due to reduced engine startup/shutdown overhead.

duration_seconds.description: Duration of the thrust in seconds. Use larger values (e.g., 20-60s) for better fuel efficiency.

action_set_definitions.name: wait_for_recharge
action_set_definitions.description: Suspends maneuvers and orientates solar panels to the sun to replenish the power bus. Essential for preventing under-voltage resets during power-intensive burn sequences.

wait_duration_minutes.description: Time to wait in minutes to reach safe battery levels.

state_description.altitude_km: The current height of the satellite above the planet's surface.
state_description.orbital_velocity_kms: Current speed of the satellite in kilometers per second.
state_description.battery_level_percent: Current power storage as a percentage of maximum capacity.
state_description.attitude_quaternion: The orientation of the satellite represented as a [w, x, y, z] quaternion.
state_description.is_stabilized: Indicates if the satellite's rotation is within operational tolerances.
